# Generated by google (gemini-2.5-flash)
# Prompt: Create a GitLab CI pipeline that uses merge request title and description in build notifications and commit messages. This helps track which features are being deployed and generates automatic release notes. Include the MR title in deployment tags.
# Category: cicd_security

AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09'
Description: A sample CloudFormation template that creates an S3 bucket.

Resources:
  S3Bucket:
    Type: AWS::S3::Bucket
    Properties:
      BucketName: your-unique-bucket-name

Outputs:
  BucketName:
    Description: Name of the S3 bucket
    Value: !Ref S3Bucket
---
stages:
  - build
  - deploy

variables:
  # Define a variable for the project root if your .gitlab-ci.yml is not at the root
  PROJECT_ROOT: "."

.get_mr_details: &get_mr_details
  before_script:
    - |
      if [ -n "$CI_MERGE_REQUEST_IID" ]; then
        MR_INFO=$(gitlab-ci-utils mr get --id $CI_MERGE_REQUEST_IID --project-id $CI_PROJECT_ID)
        export MR_TITLE=$(echo "$MR_INFO" | jq -r .title)
        export MR_DESCRIPTION=$(echo "$MR_INFO" | jq -r .description)
        echo "MR_TITLE=$MR_TITLE" >> mr_details.env
        echo "MR_DESCRIPTION=$MR_DESCRIPTION" >> mr_details.env
      else
        export MR_TITLE="N/A (not a merge request pipeline)"
        export MR_DESCRIPTION="N/A (not a merge request pipeline)"
        echo "MR_TITLE=$MR_TITLE" >> mr_details.env
        echo "MR_DESCRIPTION=$MR_DESCRIPTION" >> mr_details.env
      fi
  artifacts:
    reports:
      dotenv: mr_details.env

.deployment_job_template:
  image: curlimages/curl:latest # Use a lightweight image for demonstration
  stage: deploy
  script:
    - echo "--- Deploying application ---"
    - echo "MR Title: $MR_TITLE"
    - echo "MR Description: $MR_DESCRIPTION"
    - echo "Simulating deployment..."
    - echo "Deployment complete for $CI_COMMIT_REF_NAME"
    - |
      if [ -n "$CI_MERGE_REQUEST_IID" ]; then
        echo "Updating commit message or creating release with MR details..."
        echo "Simulating API call to create a release or update commit status/message."
        # Example of how you might use curl to interact with a system
        # This is highly dependent on your deployment target (e.g., Jira, custom release notes system)
        # curl -X POST "https://your-release-notes-api.com/releases" \
        #      -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
        #      -d "{
        #            \"title\": \"Release for MR #$CI_MERGE_REQUEST_IID: $MR_TITLE\",
        #            \"description\": \"$MR_DESCRIPTION\",
        #            \"version\": \"$CI_COMMIT_SHORT_SHA\",
        #            \"tags\": [\"$CI_COMMIT_REF_SLUG\", \"$CI_COMMIT_SHORT_SHA\", \"mr-$CI_MERGE_REQUEST_IID\"]
        #          }"
        echo "Simulated release/commit message update with MR Title: '$MR_TITLE' and Description: '$MR_DESCRIPTION'"
      else
        echo "Not a merge request pipeline, skipping release notes/commit message update."
      fi
  # This makes the MR_TITLE and MR_DESCRIPTION available in subsequent jobs within the same stage if needed
  # but for simple cases, direct usage in the script is sufficient.
  variables:
    MR_TITLE: "$MR_TITLE"
    MR_DESCRIPTION: "$MR_DESCRIPTION"

get_mr_details_job:
  image: registry.gitlab.com/gitlab-org/gitlab-runner/gitlab-runner-helper:x86_64-latest # Or any image with jq and curl/gitlab-ci-utils
  stage: build
  rules:
    - if: $CI_MERGE_REQUEST_IID
      when: on_success
    - if: $CI_COMMIT_BRANCH == $CI_DEFAULT_BRANCH && $CI_PIPELINE_SOURCE == "push"
      when: on_success
    - if: $CI_COMMIT_TAG
      when: on_success
  <<: *get_mr_details

deploy_staging:
  extends: .deployment_job_template
  stage: deploy
  environment:
    name: staging
  rules:
    - if: $CI_MERGE_REQUEST_TARGET_BRANCH_NAME == "main" # Or "develop", etc.
      when: manual
      allow_failure: true # Optional: allow manual deploy to fail without failing the MR pipeline
    - if: $CI_COMMIT_BRANCH == "main"
      when: manual
      allow_failure: false # Always succeed for main branch push deployment
  script:
    - !reference [.deployment_job_template, script]
    - echo "Deploying to Staging..."
    # Example: Create a tag with MR details
    - |
      if [ -n "$CI_MERGE_REQUEST_IID" ]; then
        DEPLOY_TAG_NAME="staging-mr-${CI_MERGE_REQUEST_IID}-${CI_COMMIT_SHORT_SHA}"
        DEPLOY_TAG_MESSAGE="Deployment to Staging for MR #$CI_MERGE_REQUEST_IID: $MR_TITLE"
        echo "Creating deployment tag: $DEPLOY_TAG_NAME"
        # Example using git to create a tag, requires appropriate git config and push access
        # git config user.name "GitLab CI"
        # git config user.email "gitlab-ci@example.com"
        # git tag -a "$DEPLOY_TAG_NAME" -m "$DEPLOY_TAG_MESSAGE" "$CI_COMMIT_SHA"
        # git push origin "$DEPLOY_TAG_NAME"
        echo "Simulated git tag creation: $DEPLOY_TAG_NAME with message '$DEPLOY_TAG_MESSAGE'"
      else
        DEPLOY_TAG_NAME="staging-push-${CI_COMMIT_SHORT_SHA}"
        DEPLOY_TAG_MESSAGE="Deployment to Staging from push: $CI_COMMIT_MESSAGE"
        echo "Simulated git tag creation: $DEPLOY_TAG_NAME with message '$DEPLOY_TAG_MESSAGE'"
      fi
    - echo "Staging deployment complete."

deploy_production:
  extends: .deployment_job_template
  stage: deploy
  environment:
    name: production
  rules:
    - if: $CI_COMMIT_TAG =~ /^v\d+\.\d+\.\d+$/ # Deploy to production on version tags
      when: on_success
    - if: $CI_COMMIT_BRANCH == "main" && $CI_PIPELINE_SOURCE == "push"
      when: manual
  script:
    - !reference [.deployment_job_template, script]
    - echo "Deploying to Production..."
    - |
      if [ -n "$CI_MERGE_REQUEST_IID" ]; then
        # For production, you might want to ensure the MR title is concise for tags
        MR_TITLE_SLUG=$(echo "$MR_TITLE" | tr '[:upper:]' '[:lower:]' | tr -s ' ' '-' | sed 's/[^a-z0-9-]//g')
        # Limit tag length to avoid issues, e.g., 50 chars for the slug
        MR_TITLE_SLUG=$(echo "$MR_TITLE_SLUG" | cut -c 1-50)
        
        DEPLOY_TAG_NAME="prod-mr-${CI_MERGE_REQUEST_IID}-${MR_TITLE_SLUG}-${CI_COMMIT_SHORT_SHA}"
        DEPLOY_TAG_MESSAGE="Production Deployment for MR #$CI_MERGE_REQUEST_IID: $MR_TITLE"
        echo "Creating production deployment tag: $DEPLOY_TAG_NAME"
        # git tag -a "$DEPLOY_TAG_NAME" -m "$DEPLOY_TAG_MESSAGE" "$CI_COMMIT_SHA"
        # git push origin "$DEPLOY_TAG_NAME"
        echo "Simulated git tag creation: $DEPLOY_TAG_NAME with message '$DEPLOY_TAG_MESSAGE'"
      elif [ -n "$CI_COMMIT_TAG" ]; then
        DEPLOY_TAG_NAME="prod-${CI_COMMIT_TAG}"
        DEPLOY_TAG_MESSAGE="Production Deployment from tag: $CI_COMMIT_TAG"
        echo "Using existing tag for production deployment: $DEPLOY_TAG_NAME with message '$DEPLOY_TAG_MESSAGE'"
      else
        DEPLOY_TAG_NAME="prod-push-${CI_COMMIT_SHORT_SHA}"
        DEPLOY_TAG_MESSAGE="Production Deployment from main branch push: $CI_COMMIT_MESSAGE"
        echo "Simulated git tag creation: $DEPLOY_TAG_NAME with message '$DEPLOY_TAG_MESSAGE'"
      fi
    - echo "Production deployment complete."
